Top Glove: 2Q Rev Rose 61% on Year
EMEA Morning Briefing: Markets Await Central Bank Decisions
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Bank of England Rate Decision, U.K. unemployment; trading updates for Hapag-Lloyd, Investec, RWE

Opening Call:

European stock futures were broadly lower after Asia stocks traded mixed. U.S. Treasury yields were lower, the dollar slightly weakened, while gold rose and oil edged higher.

Equities:

European futures pointed to a mostly lower open as investors further digested the Federal Reserve's decision to extend its pause on interest rate cuts. Investors looked ahead to the meetings of Bank of England, Swiss National Bank and Sweden's Riksbank later in the day.

The BOE is expected to leave rates on hold and continue to signal that cuts in the future will be gradual.

Economists at Nomura led by George Buckley said, "We expect the bank to remain on hold in March, before cutting again in May," and to then cut at every other meeting "between now and February 2026, inclusive, for a terminal rate of 3.5%."

Markets are expected to have a muted reaction to the central bank's decision, Danske Bank analysts said, reiterating its expectations for the BOE to maintain its guidance of gradual and cautious interest-rate cuts.

Forex:

The Bank of England's decision is unlikely to have much influence on sterling as markets have already priced in prospects of interest rates being left on hold at 4.50%, said TD Securities analysts.

Instead, sterling will probably be influenced by global developments rather than domestic factors, they said.

The BOE's guidance is "likely to remain unchanged and highlight data dependence, with the wording pointing towards continuing 'gradual' cuts, " the analysts added.

Bonds:

Market expectations over limited interest-rate cuts by the Bank of England is keeping gilt yields higher than their developed markets peers, said MUFG's Lee Hardman, adding that inflation concerns are likely to limit the pace of rate cuts.

"It will become more uncomfortable for the BOE to keep cutting rates heading into the summer when inflation is expected to temporarily pick up towards 4.0%," he said.

-

Treasury yields fell overnight as the Fed's moves broadly met expectations, with interest rates being held while officials' projection for inflation ticked up and growth forecast declined.

Estimates of two interest rate cuts this year stayed unchanged, in line with markets pricing.

Energy:

Oil edged up amid an uncertain outlook for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal, with Russia and Ukraine exchanging allegations that each side had attacked energy and oil facilities overnight.

This development came just hours after Russian President Putin agreed to a 30-day pause in attacks on energy infrastructure and Ukrainian President Zelensky also agreed to the partial cease-fire against power and energy facilities, the White House said.

"The oil market has experienced volatility in recent days, facing conflicting forces that keep traders under uncertainty," said Pepperstone research strategist Quasar Elizundia.

Metals:

Gold rose after its front-month futures set a new record high ahead of the Fed's rate decision, marking seven consecutive sessions that gold has finished higher.

Going forward for the precious metals prices, what may counterweigh the Fed's inflation uncertainty is if the peace negotiations in the Russia-Ukraine war yield a positive result, analysts said.

Safe haven demand has been the driving force for gold prices in recent sessions, they added.

-

Copper rose, breaking above the psychological threshhold of $10,000 a ton as investors braced for additional tariff announcements in the coming days.

"In the long term, tariffs could be bearish for copper and other industrial metals in the context of slowing growth and keeping inflation higher for longer," ING analysts wrote.

"With growth in the U.S. likely to slow on the back of tariffs and China already struggling to revive its economy, demand for copper and other industrial metals is likely to weaken looking ahead," they said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Fed Projections See an Economy Dramatically Reset by Trump's Election

The Federal Reserve's first set of projections since Donald Trump's inauguration underscored-in the central bank's understated and technocratic fashion-just how much the president's plans to press ahead with widespread tariffs have turned the economic outlook on its head.

Months ago, policymakers presumed they would spend 2025 gradually cutting rates to keep inflation heading down without a big rise in joblessness to achieve the so-called soft landing. The latest projections point to the prospect that tariffs covering a swath of goods and materials will send up prices while sapping investment, sentiment and growth, at least in the short run.

U.K. Is Expected to Follow Fed With Rate Pause. Why the BOE Really Wants to Cut.

The Bank of England is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged this week, in line with the Federal Reserve's action on Wednesday. It is still likely to cut rates more than the Fed later this year.

Like its U.S. counterpart, the U.K. central bank expects inflation to continue to slow over the next few months, but it wants to be sure it is on track and avoid lowering borrowing costs too quickly.

How Trump's talk with Putin about Ukraine involves this top U.S. industry

U.S. President Donald Trump bragged earlier this week that gasoline prices are down since he took office in January - proof, the White House claims, that his economic policies are working. A more accurate reason could be that the world's two biggest economies - America and China - are both slowing, thus lowering demand and pushing global prices down.

Russia Jailed Her Husband In Occupied Ukraine. Then Natalya Swung Into Action.

Natalya Barchuk edged nervously toward the prison in the Russian-occupied city of Kalanchak in southern Ukraine clutching a parcel of food and a bag of fresh clothes.

She'd been told her husband might be inside, one of thousands of civilians detained in an opaque prison system on occupied territory under the ultimate control of the Russian security service. Guards accepted the care packages but wouldn't tell her whether her Oleksiy was there.

EU Pushes Ahead With Big Tech Antitrust Enforcement

The European Commission moved forward with the enforcement of its digital antitrust rules on Apple and Alphabet's Google amid rising trade tensions with the U.S. over the treatment of American tech giants.

The commission said Wednesday that it is concerned that Google's search results display-and its terms for app developers using its Play Store on Android phones-break the rules of the Digital Markets Act by giving Google's own tech an unfair advantage over others.

SoftBank to Buy Semiconductor Company Ampere Computing for $6.5 Billion

SoftBank Group inked a deal to purchase Ampere Computing, a fabless semiconductor company, for $6.5 billion in cash.

Japanese investment holding company SoftBank said Wednesday that the purchase would help to enhance its capabilities in key artificial-intelligence areas and accelerate its growth initiatives. The deal comes as the company is broadening its AI-infrastructure investments.

Bondi's Campaign Against Domestic Terrorism Starts With Tesla Foes

Teslas set on fire. Bullets and bombs aimed at showrooms and charging stations. Is it terrorism?

Attorney General Pam Bondi thinks so, declaring a Justice Department crackdown on what she views as politically motivated attacks against Tesla leader Elon Musk, one of President Trump's most powerful advisers.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Thursday

00:01/UK: BRC Consumer Sentiment Monitor

05:30/NED: Feb Unemployment

07:00/UK: Feb UK monthly unemployment figures

07:00/SWE: 4Q Financial accounts

07:00/SWI: Feb Trade Balance

07:00/GER: Feb PPI

08:30/SWI: Swiss National Bank monetary policy assessment

08:30/SWE: Swedish repo rate announcement

09:00/POL: Feb Average gross wages

09:00/POL: Feb Industrial Production Index

09:00/POL: Feb PPI

10:00/LUX: Feb Unemployment

10:00/EU: Jan Construction output

11:00/UK: Feb Aluminium Production report

11:00/TUR: Turkish interest rate decision

11:00/UK: Mar CBI Industrial Trends Survey

12:00/UK: Agents' Summary of Business Conditions

12:00/UK: UK interest rate decision

17:59/POR: Jan ICSG Copper Report

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Fitch Keeps British American Tobacco's Rating, Outlook on Strong Business Profile
data.description:
Fitch on Wednesday affirmed British American Tobacco's (, BTI.JO) long-term issuer default rating at BBB+, with a stable outlook.

The rating affirmation considers the UK-based tobacco group's strong business profile, vast regional and brand diversification, and conservative financial policy. It also reflects the company's ability to boost its portfolio of next-generation products as positive for the rating.

Fitch expects the company to generate strong free cash flow annually from 2026 to 2028, driven by low-to mid-single-digit organic revenue growth.

data.title: India's Paras Defence gains on bagging 1.4 billion rupee order
data.description:
** India's Paras Defence and Space Technologies jumps as much as 10% to one-month high of 1,047.7 rupees, last up 6.6%

** PRAF on course for third consecutive session of gains

** Co receives order worth 1.42 billion rupees ($16.5 million) for development of laser source module and integration with beam control system on mobile platform

** PRAF top pct gainer on Nifty India Defence (.NIFTYINDDEFENCE) index, which is down 0.4%

** More than 650,000 shares change hands in an hour's trade, 4x the six-month avg volume

** PRAF up 4% YTD, turns positive with day's gains

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Dow Climbs After FOMC Interest-Rate Decision

Stocks rose after the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, while lowering its forecasts for growth this year.

Fed Projections See an Economy Dramatically Reset by Trump's Election

Not long ago, Fed officials presumed that 2025 would simply be about getting to the soft landing.

U.K. Is Expected to Follow Fed With Rate Pause. Why the BOE Really Wants to Cut.

The British economy has been much weaker than that of the U.S., and inflation is still well above the BOE target.

How Trump's talk with Putin about Ukraine involves this top U.S. industry

Trump wants Europe to buy more of America's liquified natural gas. But tariffs get in the way.

Australian Job Market Strength Showing Cracks

Australia's economy shed jobs in February in what could be the first clear indication in some time that interest rates have remained too high for too long.

New Zealand's Economy Returns to Growth; Outlook Uncertain

New Zealand's economy exited an extended period of recession in the final months of last year supported by hefty cuts in official interest rates, but the path ahead is uncertain for the small commodity-exporting nation given recent downgrades to world growth and a widening global trade war.

Canadian Consumers, Retailers Change Tack to Counter Tariff Stresses

Canadian consumers are responding to U.S. tariffs and presidential musings of annexation with a "Buy Canadian" movement that retailers and producers are swiftly embracing.

The Fed's interest-rate pause has a silver lining for worried Americans - if they make this move

Consumers are increasingly concerned about their jobs and their access to credit as recession fears loom.

Bonds Are Holding Up, but the Playbook Is Changing

As stocks slid in recent weeks, bond prices have ticked up, offsetting some losses

EU Targets 15% Cut in Steel Imports Amid U.S. Tariffs, Global Oversupply

The European Commission will protect the bloc's steel industry and introduce long-term measures to preserve its competitiveness in an industry awash in overcapacity globally.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Trump Considers Extending Chevron License to Pump Oil in Venezuela

Chief Executive Mike Wirth lobbied top administration officials including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

Building-Products Distributor QXO Clinches Deal for Beacon Roofing

The agreement comes after a hostile takeover attempt.

SoftBank to Buy Ampere Computing for $6.5 Billion

The deal is the latest in a series of investments by the Japanese technology investment company to advance its artificial intelligence initiatives..

Bondi's Campaign Against Domestic Terrorism Starts With Tesla Foes

The attorney general is declaring a Justice Department crackdown on what she views as politically motivated attacks against Tesla CEO Elon Musk.

Crypto Exchange Kraken Nears $1.5 Billion Deal for Futures Trading Business

NinjaTrader, a futures trading platform, would expand Kraken's offerings.

Ford Looks to Innovate Faster With AI Agents and Nvidia GPUs

Bryan Goodman, Ford Motor's director of artificial intelligence, said he's pushing forward a number of use cases for artificial intelligence at the company, as part of an effort to design and engineer new cars as quickly as its competitors.

Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner Resigns

The passenger railroad's chief executive is leaving the post after a little more than three years in the role.

Nissan to Cut 20% of Top Management Positions

Nissan said its top management positions will transition to a single-layer, non-officer framework, under which all corporate officers will assume the title of corporate executive.

Ben & Jerry's Says CEO Ousted by Unilever Over Brand's Social Activism

In a court filing, the ice cream maker said Unilever removed David Stever as CEO without approval from its independent board.

EU Pushes Ahead With Big Tech Antitrust Enforcement

The European Commission moved forward with enforcement of its digital antitrust rules on Apple and Alphabet's Google amid rising trade tensions with the U.S. over treatment of American tech giants.

data.title: India's Paytm, One Mobikwik Systems fall after lower UPI incentives
data.description:
** Shares of Paytm fall 4.6% to 727.80 rupees, One Mobikwik Systems down 5% to 316.90 rupees

** India's union cabinet approved incentive of 15 bln rupees ($174 mln) for low-value UPI transactions between April 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025

** UPI is a real-time payment system to transfer money between bank accounts

** Brokerage Jefferies said government's incentives for low-value UPI transactions are half of last year's despite rise in such transactions

** Introduction of merchant charges (MDR) on UPI transactions could help Paytm in offsetting impact from reduction in UPI incentives, Jefferies says

** Paytm set to fall after gaining for three straight sessions, down ~28% YTD

** ONEM down ~45% in 2025

($1 = 86.2220 Indian rupees)

data.title: AFFIN Bank, MUFG Bank (Malaysia) to Collaborate on Key Sectors
data.description:
AFFIN Bank signed a memorandum of understanding with MUFG Bank (Malaysia) to cooperate in sectors such as semiconductors, healthcare, green transition, and digital transformation.

Both lenders will also focus on supporting economic growth in key regions identified by the Malaysian government, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

The MOU will be effective for three years or until terminated by either party, the filing added.

data.title: Novartis Ends Diverse Hiring Panels in US
data.description:
Novartis decided to discontinue diverse panels for hiring in the US, Reuters reported Wednesday.

The Swiss drugmaker attributed the change to increasing legal restrictions and policy shifts affecting diversity, equity, and inclusion, or DEI, programs in the US, but told the news outlet in a statement that it still believes in "embracing varied perspectives and fostering equal opportunity for all of our people."

US President Donald Trump publicly challenged the legality of certain DEI practices and indicated potential investigations into companies' compliance with relevant regulations, Reuters wrote.

data.title: EU investigates BYD's Hungary plant for potential unfair Chinese subsidies, FT reports
data.description: The European Union is probing whether China provided unfair subsidies for a BYD electric car plant in Hungary, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Indonesian stocks extend gains; Asian equities, FX climb on Fed rate outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indonesia stocks rise up to 2%
  • Rupiah gains after three sessions of losses
  • Singapore shares up 0.8%, Taiwan stocks jump 1.8%

Indonesia's shares rose and the rupiah strengthened on Thursday following the central bank's decision to stand pat on interest rates, while other Asian markets and currencies gained on the prospect that the Federal Reserve may cut rates twice this year.

Shares in Jakarta extended gains, climbing as much as 2%, after experiencing their biggest drop in nearly three years on Tuesday.

The rupiah , Asia's worst-performing currency so far this year, firmed 0.3% to 16,470 per dollar, marking its first gain in four sessions.

Bank Indonesia (BI) kept policy rates unchanged on Wednesday amid recent market turmoil, which saw investors withdrawing from stocks and selling the country's high-yielding bonds due to concerns over fiscal health and weakening domestic demand.

Indonesia's parliament passed controversial revisions to the military law, granting more civilian positions to military officers.

"In an environment of high macro policy and political uncertainty, the risk of outflows from the equity market remains visible – not only from foreign but also resident investors," Citi analysts said in a note.

Other regional stock markets tracked overnight gains in Wall Street, with equities in Taiwan TWSE:TAIEX jumping 1.8%.

Singapore's stocks rose 0.8% to a near one-month high, while South Korean shares gained 0.6%.

The U.S. Fed kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged overnight, but projected likely two quarter-point interest rate cuts later this year, calming investor nerves.

Even though U.S. policymakers expect faster-than-expected price increases, partly due to President Donald Trump's plans to levy duties on imports from trading partners, Chair Jerome Powell's belief that tariff-driven inflation will be "transitory" and mainly confined to this year helped boost risk sentiment.

In contrast, Nomura analysts said that even before Trump tariffs are fully implemented, disinflation is widespread in Asia.

"As tariffs get implemented, downside risks to growth and inflation will likely become more material."

Analysts said that, along with high real rates, there is still room for easing by Asian central banks, especially in Thailand, India, the Philippines, and Korea.

Regional currencies rose against the dollar, which remained steady but hovered close to the five-month low touched earlier this week.

The Malaysian ringgit and the South Korean won added 0.2% each.

Elsewhere, the Turkish lira closed down 2.6% on Wednesday after weakening to a record low of 42 per dollar, and the benchmark stock index fell the most in four years after authorities detained President Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** China holds benchmark lending rates steady, as expected

** Hong Kong central bank keeps base rate unchanged as Fed holds steady

data.title: Winro Commercial (India) to Acquire Additional 4.95% Stake in Saraswati Commercial
data.description:
Financial services company Winro Commercial (India) has agreed to acquire an additional 4.95% stake in non-banking finance company Saraswati Commercial (India) for 786.3 million Indian rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company will acquire 66,000 equity shares of Saraswati Commercial at 11,913 rupees per share, which includes a premium of 11,903 rupees per share, through a preferential issue.

The company's shareholding in Saraswati Commercial will increase to 22.77% from 17.82% following completion.

The investment is being done to support Saraswati Commercial's growth and capitalize on future opportunities.

data.title: Palm Oil Rises Further Above MYR 4,400
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures rose around 1% to above MYR 4,400 per tonne, extending gains for a second session after fresh data from Statistics Malaysia showed palm oil exports surged 31.8% yoy in February.

Meanwhile, the Malaysian Palm Oil Council (MPOC) noted that weaker demand from key buyers like India and China was partially offset by growing interest from Sub-Saharan Africa.

The MPOC also projected prices to range between MYR 4,400 and MYR 4,600 in March.

In top producer Indonesia, crude and refined palm oil exports jumped 62.2% mom in February to a four-month high, as Jakarta’s efforts to cut export taxes attracted buyers away from Malaysia.

Additionally, Indonesia reportedly plans to raise its palm oil export levy to 4.5%-10% of the crude palm oil reference price, up from 3%-7.5%, to support higher biodiesel blending.

However, further gains were capped by weak exports, as data from cargo surveyors noted a drop of 7.5% to 10.1% in shipments during the first 15 days of March.

data.title: DLF Target Price Set at INR700 by Nomura>532868.BY
DLF Started at Neutral by Nomura >532868.BY
Indonesian stocks extend gains; Asian equities, FX climb on Fed rate outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indonesia stocks rise up to 2%
  • Rupiah gains after three sessions of losses
  • Singapore shares up 0.8%, Taiwan stocks jump 1.8%

Indonesia's shares rose and the rupiah strengthened on Thursday following the central bank's decision to stand pat on interest rates, while other Asian markets and currencies gained on the prospect that the Federal Reserve may cut rates twice this year.

Shares in Jakarta extended gains, climbing as much as 2%, after experiencing their biggest drop in nearly three years on Tuesday.

The rupiah , Asia's worst-performing currency so far this year, firmed 0.3% to 16,470 per dollar, marking its first gain in four sessions.

Bank Indonesia (BI) kept policy rates unchanged on Wednesday amid recent market turmoil, which saw investors withdrawing from stocks and selling the country's high-yielding bonds due to concerns over fiscal health and weakening domestic demand.

Indonesia's parliament passed controversial revisions to the military law, granting more civilian positions to military officers.

"In an environment of high macro policy and political uncertainty, the risk of outflows from the equity market remains visible – not only from foreign but also resident investors," Citi analysts said in a note.

Other regional stock markets tracked overnight gains in Wall Street, with equities in Taiwan TWSE:TAIEX jumping 1.8%.

Singapore's stocks rose 0.8% to a near one-month high, while South Korean shares gained 0.6%.

The U.S. Fed kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged overnight, but projected likely two quarter-point interest rate cuts later this year, calming investor nerves.

Even though U.S. policymakers expect faster-than-expected price increases, partly due to President Donald Trump's plans to levy duties on imports from trading partners, Chair Jerome Powell's belief that tariff-driven inflation will be "transitory" and mainly confined to this year helped boost risk sentiment.

In contrast, Nomura analysts said that even before Trump tariffs are fully implemented, disinflation is widespread in Asia.

"As tariffs get implemented, downside risks to growth and inflation will likely become more material."

Analysts said that, along with high real rates, there is still room for easing by Asian central banks, especially in Thailand, India, the Philippines, and Korea.

Regional currencies rose against the dollar, which remained steady but hovered close to the five-month low touched earlier this week.

The Malaysian ringgit and the South Korean won added 0.2% each.

Elsewhere, the Turkish lira closed down 2.6% on Wednesday after weakening to a record low of 42 per dollar, and the benchmark stock index fell the most in four years after authorities detained President Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** China holds benchmark lending rates steady, as expected

** Hong Kong central bank keeps base rate unchanged as Fed holds steady

data.title: HK-listed HUTCHMED jumps on 2024 profit beat
data.description:
** Pharmaceuticals group HUTCHMED (China) jumps 10.7% to HK$26.45, highest since March 7

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct rise since March 13, 2024; third-biggest pct gainer on healthcare index (.HSCIH), which is up 0.5%

** Co's 2024 net income attributable came in at $37.73 mln vs $100.78 mln in 2023

** Analysts on avg had forecast loss of $58.57 mln

** Jefferies ("buy") says co's FY24 profitability ahead of guidance

** YTD, stock up 16%

data.title: Yuan eases as China sets weakest fix in two months
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dollar decline helps relieve yuan depreciation pressure
  • PBOC keen to prevent sharp gains in yuan
  • Market monitoring Sino-US trade developments

The yuan eased against the U.S. dollar on Thursday after China sets its official midpoint rate at the weakest in two months, signalling authorities are keen to prevent quick or sharp gains in the currency against a declining greenback.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.1754 per dollar, its weakest level since U.S. President Donald Trump's inauguration on January 20.

The U.S. dollar has been on the backfoot for weeks hobbled by Trump's erratic tariff policies and soft economic data.

That has helped the yuan pare losses sustained since his election in November and provided the PBOC with some reprieve from using the fixing to contain losses.

The dollar index has dropped about 4% this month, but the yuan has appreciated less than 1% against the dollar.

The weaker fixing did not mean the PBOC has deviated from its policy stance of preserving currency stability, especially as Trump's review of trade partners' currency policies in early April is coming up, said Ken Cheung, director of currency strategy at Mizuho Securities.

"The US dollar decline and Fed's easing bias should provide relief to the renminbi (yuan) market, giving the PBOC some flexibility to dial back some yuan fixing support," he said.

The spot yuan opened at 7.2250 per dollar and was last trading at 7.2363 as of 0402 GMT, 83 pips lower than the previous late session close and 0.85% weaker than the midpoint.

The dollar was steady on Thursday, hovering near a five-month low, after the Federal Reserve kept its benchmark overnight rate steady on Wednesday and indicated it was not in a rush to cut rates even as uncertainty around U.S. tariffs weighed.

Meanwhile, China held benchmark lending rates steady for the fifth straight month in March, matching market expectations.

Traders said the recent movements in the yuan suggested it is unlikely to strengthen beyond 7.22 per dollar without further market news, particularly regarding developments in trade negotiations between the world's two largest economies.

Global investment banks have raised their projections for China's economic growth and eased bearish views on the yuan, spurred by early signs of economic recovery, though concerns over escalating trade disputes with the U.S. persist.

The offshore yuan traded at 7.2391 yuan per dollar , down about 0.12% in Asian trade.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against six currencies, stood flat at 103.38.

data.title: HSS Engineers, Tenaga Nasional Sign Solar Power Purchase Deal
data.description:
HSS Engineers via its special-purpose joint venture Unique HEB Energy, signed a solar power purchase deal with Tenaga Nasional .

Unique HEB Energy is a partnership between HEBE and Unique Green Energy, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

Under the deal, the joint venture will design, build, own, and operate a 95 megawatt alternating current solar photovoltaic facility in Perak, Malaysia.

The facility is scheduled to begin operations on Oct. 11, 2027, and will span 21 years from the commercial operation date.
